steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage builds to a logical conclusion: observational data can't constrain Titan's methane mole fraction range, so the IPSL model's developers responded by prescribing a single uniform value. The blank must follow from that fact.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'further observations may clarify the fraction sufficiently for a single value': reverses the logic — the passage says sparse data forced a uniform value, not that better data would justify one. ✗
  • Choice B — 'some disagreements between the model's simulations and Titan's actual precipitation and humidity are to be expected': directly follows from the setup. A uniform methane value can't capture real variation, so model–reality disagreements are predictable. ✓
  • Choice C — 'inconsistencies across the model's simulations could be attributable to variations in the methane mole fraction': talks about internal inconsistencies within the model's own outputs, which the passage does not raise. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Titan's real methane mole fraction is likely higher than the model's value': speculates about which direction the real value differs — nothing in the passage supports this. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
